mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, consumer spending growth decelerates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicate strong consumer demand supported by accommodative policy, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observed months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving corporate capacity utilization and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week specifically during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a directional hit rate below 45% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage bills, which mechanically supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches 250.0, inflation expectations anchor higher, forcing markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening and reduced future earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months following a CPIAUCSL breach above 250.0, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside falling unemployment indicate synchronized strength in the construction sector and labor market, creating a robust demand environment that pushes S&P 500 forward returns positive over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and UNRATE signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms sustained aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during stable monetary policy environments.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a directional accuracy below 40% during periods of stable interest rates.
